ingredients.item: 4 cups (400 g) seedless black grapes, plus more to taste
ingredients.original: 4 cups (400 g) seedless black grapes, plus more to taste

ingredients.item: ¾ cup (40 g) packed fresh mint leaves
ingredients.original: ¾ cup (40 g) packed fresh mint leaves

ingredients.item: 1 Add the grapes, water, and mint leaves to a high-powered blender and blend for a few seconds, or until the ingredients are juiced.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the grapes, water, and mint leaves to a high-powered blender and blend for a few seconds, or until the ingredients are juiced.

ingredients.item: 2 Add 1 cup ice cubes and blend for a few more seconds. Taste and adjust; if it is too watery, add more grapes and blend again.
ingredients.original: 2 Add 1 cup ice cubes and blend for a few more seconds. Taste and adjust; if it is too watery, add more grapes and blend again.

ingredients.item: 3 Place a fine-mesh strainer over a large bowl or jug and pour the juice through it to strain out the large bits of grape skin.
ingredients.original: 3 Place a fine-mesh strainer over a large bowl or jug and pour the juice through it to strain out the large bits of grape skin.

ingredients.item: 4 Pour the strained juice into a pitcher and either refrigerate or serve immediately over more ice cubes. Garnish with fresh mint leaves.
ingredients.original: 4 Pour the strained juice into a pitcher and either refrigerate or serve immediately over more ice cubes. Garnish with fresh mint leaves.
